name: Into the Market
description: The archway opens onto the south end of the market square. The noise and bustle grow louder ahead.

name: The Gate
description: The stone archway back to the market entrance.

name: Market Square
description: The square opens up ahead, thronging with people and stalls.

name: South End
description: The quieter south end of the square, near the gate.

name: Rawstock Row
description: A narrow lane lined with materials vendors branches off to the east.

name: The Open Forge
description: The glow of a forge and the sound of hammering draw you west, under a heavy canvas awning.

name: Oddments Alley
description: A narrow side passage angles off to the northeast, quieter than the main square.

name: The Rendering Yards
description: The cobblestones give way to iron plate. The noise gets louder and more industrial.

name: Market Square
description: The lane opens back out into the bustle of the market square.

name: Tiv's Materials
description: A cramped stall ahead, shelves crammed with bins and jars.

name: Loading End
description: The lane continues east, the stalls giving way to larger bins and stacked crates.

exit.destinationIntent: The back end of Rawstock Row — bulk materials, wholesale vendors, maybe a warehouse or loading dock where goods arrive from other dimensions. More industrial, less retail. Might hint at how materials actually get to the market.

name: Rawstock Row
description: Back out into the narrow lane of materials vendors.

name: Market Square
description: Back into the open market square.

name: Market Square
description: The passage opens back into the busy market square.

name: The Fair Exchange
description: The alley opens into a quieter court. The stalls here look more permanent.

name: Oddments Alley
description: Back through the narrow passage toward the main market.

name: The Broker's Nook
description: A small stall at the back, walled in ledgers.

name: The Fair Exchange
description: Back out into the open court.

name: Market Square
description: Back into the bustle of the market square. The cobblestones feel civilized after the iron plate.

name: The Smelting Bay
description: The heat intensifies ahead. Crucibles glow in a wide workspace.

name: The Rendering Yards
description: Back toward the entrance. The air cools slightly.

name: The Nut Cart
description: A brass cart tended by a vendor with too many elbows. Smells good.

name: Market Square, South End
description: Back into the south end of the square.

name: Wire & Filament
description: A stall draped in coils of wire, run by a vendor who doesn't look up.

name: Rawstock Row
description: Back out into the lane.

name: Food Row
description: Steam and the smell of cooking rise from stalls to the northwest.

name: Market Square
description: Back into the main square.

name: The Lens Grinder
description: A workbench under a canvas awning, covered in glass dust.

name: Market Square, South End
description: Back into the south end of the square.

name: The Bindery
description: A stall hung with sheets of pressed fiber. Thread glows at the needle.

name: The Fair Exchange
description: Back into the court.

name: The Press House
description: A shed-sized machine cranks and hisses to the east.

name: The Rendering Yards
description: Back toward the entrance.

name: The Tea Stall
description: A quieter spot further along, where steam rises from cups that glow faintly.

exit.destinationIntent: A drink stall or tea house deeper in Food Row. Serves hot drinks from different dimensions — some glow, some steam in unusual colors. A quieter spot where vendors come to sit and talk. Maybe a vendor who trades in rumors along with beverages.

name: The Spice Stall
description: Bins of powders in colors you didn't know existed. The air prickles.

exit.destinationIntent: A spice vendor's stall — bins of powders and dried things from dozens of dimensions. Some spices have mild material properties (a spice that makes things temporarily magnetic, one that changes color when it touches something from a specific dimension). The vendor organizes by effect, not by origin.

name: The Sound Vendor
description: Glass jars line the shelves. One of them is humming.

exit.destinationIntent: A stall selling sounds in jars — corked glass bottles that emit noises when opened. Some are useful (a jar of silence for sleeping in a noisy market), some are strange (the sound of a door closing in a dimension that doesn't have doors). The vendor collects sounds the way others collect materials.

name: The Time Merchant
description: A stall where hourglasses line the shelves, each filled with differently colored sand.

exit.destinationIntent: A stall deeper in the Fair Exchange where the vendor trades in time — literally. They'll buy an hour of your future and sell you an hour of someone else's past. The transactions are precise and always fair. The vendor keeps time in small hourglasses filled with sand of different colors.

name: The Fiber Works
description: A spinning wheel whirs. Thread of impossible thinness accumulates on spools.

exit.destinationIntent: A fiber workshop where raw dimensional materials are drawn into thread and rope. A vendor operates a spinning wheel that pulls material through a dimensional aperture to thin it. The resulting fibers have unusual properties depending on the source material. More craft-focused than the smelting bay, dealing in flexible materials rather than rigid ones.

name: The Quench Yard
description: Vats of liquid line the wall. Steam rises from one. Another is perfectly still.

exit.destinationIntent: A quenching yard — vats of different liquids for cooling hot materials. Some vats contain water, some contain oils, one contains a liquid that isn't quite there. The properties of the quenching medium affect the final material. An old worker tends the vats and knows which liquid to use for what.
